introduction.p: Ray is a runner, and set out this morning on a jog with a goal to reach a nearby summit and return home in under one hour. They jogged a steady uphill path to the top of the nearby summit, and it took 35 min to get there. Now jogging home, they will run a distance of 2.5 mi. What will their rate (in minutes per mile) need to be in order to make the goal of returning home in under 60 minutes?

statement.p: Write an inequality to represent this situation, using x to represent Rays jogging rate in minutes per mile.
Solve this inequality. Rays jogging rate needs to beat what rate?
Use interval notation to express what Rays jogging rate (in minutes per mile) might be.
